column16: Specifier for a value range in the format ( START ; END ).  START and END values are optional. [ and ] can also be used as brackets  to indicate inclusion of the relevant range endpoint.
column20: Value to be assigned to corresponding range
column24: Value to be assigned to pixels outside any range (defaults to 0)

column6: Returns a collection of point features for the pixels of a raster.  The band values are provided as attributes.
column12: CRS in which the points will be displayed
column16: scale
column20: interpolation
column24: Add Emishpere

column6: Computes statistics for the distribution of a certain quantity in a set of polygonal zones.
column8: Input raster to compute statistics for
column12: Source band used to compute statistics (default is 0)
column16: Zone polygon features for which to compute statistics
column20: Raster whose values will be used as classes for the statistical analysis. Each zone reports statistics partitioned by classes according to the values of the raster. Must be a single band raster with integer values.

column6: Returns a scaled and translated version of a given raster
column12: Scale factor along the x axis
column16: Scale factor along the y axis
column20: Offset along the x axis
column24: Offset along the y axis
column28: Interpolation function to use.  Values are NEAREST  BILINEAR  BICUBIC2  BICUBIC

column6: Styles a raster using a given SLD and raster symbolizer.
column12: Styled Layer Descriptor (SLD) style containing a raster symbolizer

column6: Fill transparent pixels
column12: Width inside which searching for nearest pixel value

column6: Create the full skeleton line of a Polygon
column8: The Geometry to extract the center line from
column12: %age of perimeter to use for densification/simplification of input/output. Optional  default is 5.0

column6: Computes one or more aggregation functions on a feature attribute. Functions include Count  Average  Max  Median  Min  StdDev  and Sum.
column12: Attribute on which to perform aggregation
column16: An aggregate function to compute. Functions include Count  Average  Max  Median  Min  StdDev  Sum and SumArea.
column20: If True computes all aggregation values in a single pass (this will defeat DBMS-specific optimizations)
column24: List of group by attributes

column6: Uses Barnes Analysis to compute an interpolated surface over a set of irregular data points.
column12: Name of attribute containing the data value to be interpolated
column16: Limit for the number of input features processed
column20: Length scale for the interpolation  in units of the source data CRS
column24: Convergence factor for refinement (between 0 and 1  default 0.3)
column28: Number of passes to compute (default = 2)
column32: Minimum number of observations required to support a grid cell (default = 2)
column36: Maximum distance to an observation for it to support a grid cell  in units of the source CRS (default = 0  meaning all observations used)
column40: Value to use for NO_DATA cells (default = -999)
column44: Resolution of the computed grid in pixels per grid cell (default = 1)
column48: Distance to expand the query envelope by  in units of the source CRS (larger values provide a more stable surface)
column56: Width of the output raster in pixels
column60: Height of the output raster in pixels

column6: Computes the bounding box of the input features.
Buffers features by a distance value supplied either as a parameter or by a feature attribute. Calculates buffers based on Cartesian distances.
column12: Fixed value to use for the buffer distance
column16: Attribute containing the buffer distance value

column6: Computes the geometric centroids of features
Computes a new attribute to classify another attribute by intervals over vector data sets.
column12: Attribute to be classified using intervals of values.
column16: List of thresholds (use this one to specify custom intervals). Ignored if classifier is specified (use classes in that case).
column20: List of class values for each given threshold (+1 for out of range).
column24: Classifier type (EqualInterval  Quantile  Jenks  etc.). Use with classes to calculate intervals automatically
column28: Classifier # of classes  used when classifier is specified (defaults to 5).
column32: Include or exclude current threshold in the interval.
column36: Name of the output attribute with class values (defaults to class).
column40: Optional binding type for output values (defaults to String).

column6: Clips (crops) features to a given geometry
column12: Geometry to use for clipping (in same CRS as input features)
column16: Attempt to preserve Z values from the original geometry (interpolate value for new points)

column6: Collects the default geometries of the input features and combines them into a single geometry collection
Computes the number of features in a feature collection.
Converts a geometry into a feature collection.
column12: Coordinate reference system of the input geometry (if not provided in the geometry)
column16: Feauturetype name for the feature collection

column6: Calculates statistics from feature values classified into bins/classes.
column12: The feature attribute to analyze
column16: The statistics to calculate for each class
column20: The number of breaks/classes
column24: The classification method
column28: The attribute value to be omitted from any calculation

column6: Generates a georeferenced regular grid of cells.  Output contains the attributes: cell - the cell polygon; id - a unique identifier; centerX and centerY - the ordinates of the cell center.

column16: Height of a cell (in units of the grid CRS).  Only for rectangular grid  defaults to equal width.
column20: Distance between vertices along cell sides (in units of the grid CRS)
column24: Type of grid to be generated.  Specifies shape of cells in grid.

column6: Computes a heatmap surface over a set of data points and outputs as a single-band raster.
column12: Radius of the density kernel in pixels
column16: Name of the attribute to use for data point weight
column20: Resolution at which to compute the heatmap (in pixels). Default = 1
column24: Bounding box of the output
column28: Width of output raster in pixels
column32: Height of output raster in pixels

column6: Returns a feature collection consisting of the features from the first collection which are spatially contained in at least one feature of the second collection.
column12: Second feature collection

column6: Spatial intersection of two feature collections  including combining attributes from both.
column12: Second feature collection
column16: First feature collection attribute to include
column20: Second feature collection attribute to include
column24: Specifies geometry computed for intersecting features.  INTERSECTION (default) computes the spatial intersection of the inputs. FIRST copies geometry A.  SECOND copies geometry B.
column28: Indicates whether to output feature area percentages (attributes percentageA and percentageB)
column32: Indicates whether to output feature areas (attributes areaA and areaB)

column16: Attribute providing end measure of feature

column12: Attribute providing start measure of feature

column12: Attribute providing start measure of feature
column16: Attribute providing end measure of feature
column20: Measure for start of segment to extract
column24: Measure for end of segment to extract

column6: Returns the feature in a given feature collection that has the smallest distance to a given point.

column16: Coordinate reference system of the collection and point (default is the input collection CRS)

column6: Returns a collection of circular buffer polygons with specified radii centered on a given point
column12: Coordinate reference system of the point and the generated buffer polygons

column40: Optional CQL filter to restrict the points to be clustered

column6: Queries a feature collection using an optional filter and an optional list of attributes to include.  Can also be used to convert feature collection format.

column16: Attempt to preserve Z values from the original geometry (interpolate value for new points)

column16: Target coordinate reference system to use for reprojection

column16: Coordinate reference system to assume for input geometry (default is to use the input collection CRS)

column6: Returns single feature collection containing all features from two input feature collections.  The output attribute schema is a combination of the attributes from the inputs.  Attributes with same name but different types will be converted to strings.

column28: Bounding box of the area to rasterize

column6: Computes statistics for the distribution of a given attribute in a set of polygonal zones.  Input must be points.
